title: Tap Into Energy Momentum With These 3 Stocks
text: The Zacks Oils and Energy sector has displayed notable relative strength over the last three months, adding +12% compared to the S&P 500's modest gain. Rising energy prices have helped shift sentiment, with buyers stepping up after a rough first half.

title: ConocoPhillips: Payout Is Set To Rise Due To M&A And Higher Oil Prices
text: ConocoPhillips shares have seen a strong run since July, driven by higher crude oil prices. The company's Q2 earnings were down due to lower realizations, but rising oil prices should support future earnings growth. ConocoPhillips has strong cash flow, made a shrewd acquisition, and favorable commodity prices, making it a good investment opportunity.

title: 3 High-Yield Energy Stocks That Are Gushing Gobs of Free Cash Flow
text: ConocoPhillips is the safest way to play the oil and gas market. Despite the recent dividend cut, Devon Energy is still attractive for income-seeking investors.

title: ConocoPhillips: OPEC+ Supply Cuts Could Translate To Profit Upside
text: ConocoPhillips benefits from OPEC+ supply cuts, which could lead to higher earnings and free cash flow in the near term. COP is set to profit from the uptrend in petroleum prices. ConocoPhillips is trading at 12X forward earnings and has a significant stock buyback plan in place.

title: ConocoPhillips to Hold Third-Quarter Earnings Conference Call on Thursday, Nov. 2

title: California sues Chevron, Exxon, Shell, others for public deception on climate change
text: The state claims that big oil companies have intentionally covered up their role in exacerbating climate change since at least the 1970s.

title: BP, Exxon, Shell, Chevron sued by California for decades of lies
text: Exxon, BP, Shell, ConocoPhillips (NYSE:COP) and Chevron are being sued by the State of California, which claims they have lied for decades about the extent of the damage likely to be caused by using oil and gas. A civil suit filed in the state Superior Court in San Francisco wants the companies to pay into a fund to repair damage caused by wildfires and storms that have recently ravaged the region.

title: The 3 Most Undervalued Energy Stocks to Buy in September 2023
text: Whether it's September, October or any other month, the game is always the same. Identify undervalued stocks, put your capital to work and reap the rewards of your patience as they increase in value at some later point.

title: California Sues 5 Oil Giants: Here Are The Biggest Numbers Behind The Explosive Lawsuit
text: California Attorney General Rob Bonta accuses several oil companies of lying to the public “to further their record-breaking profits.”

title: California goes to war with oil and gas giants over climate change 'deception'
text: The state of California has filed a lawsuit against major oil and gas companies, alleging the fossil fuel industry has lied about the harms of climate change for decades.

title: California sues oil giants for downplaying risks posed by fossil fuels - NYT
text: The state of California has sued major oil companies including Exxon Mobil Corp , Shell PLC , and Chevron Corp , accusing them of playing down the risks posed by fossil fuels, the New York Times reported on Friday.

title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Signs LNG Deal at Netherlands Gate Terminal
text: ConocoPhillips' (COP) agreement with the Gate LNG terminal involves a capacity of 1.5 million tons per annum for natural gas or 2 billion cubic meters of gas.

title: ConocoPhillips signs 15-year LNG deal at Gate terminal in Netherlands
text: ConocoPhillips on Thursday said it has signed a 15-year liquefied natural gas (LNG) throughput deal in the Netherlands, starting in 2031, to secure additional regasification capacity in Europe.

title: ConocoPhillips to Further Diversify Global LNG Portfolio with Additional Long-Term Agreement for European Regas Capacity
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--ConocoPhillips announced today the signing of agreement to secure additional regasification capacity at the Gate LNG terminal in the Netherlands.

title: Oil Prices Are on the Rise: 3 Dividend Stocks to Play the Energy Rally

title: Biden administration burnishes its green credentials with Alaska move
text: In a significant move to bolster its environmental agenda, the Biden administration has cancelled oil leases in the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge (ANWR), initially awarded during the final days of the Trump era. The leases were suspended six months ago and have now been formally revoked due to "multiple legal deficiencies," according to the Interior Department.

title: 3 Integrated US Energy Stocks Set to Escape Industry Weakness
text: Extreme volatility in oil and gas prices, and high input price in refining activities are making the outlook for the Zacks Oil & Gas US Integrated industry gloomy. ConocoPhillips (COP), Occidental (OXY) and Hess (HES) are likely to survive the business challenges.
